info.title: Dokploy API
info.description: Complete API documentation for Dokploy - Deploy applications, manage databases, and orchestrate your infrastructure. This API allows you to programmatically manage all aspects of your Dokploy instance.

post.description: Update the monitoring configuration for the web server including refresh rates, thresholds, and container services. Restarts the monitoring system and returns the updated settings. Disabled on cloud.
Creates a new application in the specified project environment. Supports GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Git, Docker image, and drop sources.

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about an application by its ID, including git provider access status and deployment configuration.

post.description: Restarts the Docker container for the application by mechanizing it. Resets the application status to idle, then to done on success or error on failure.
Permanently deletes an application and cleans up all associated resources including Docker services, Traefik configuration, deployments, middlewares, and source code.
Stops the running Docker service for the application and sets its status to idle.
Starts the Docker service for the application and sets its status to done.
Triggers a rebuild and redeployment of the application. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.
Updates the environment variables, build arguments, and build secrets for an application.
Updates the build type and related settings for an application, including Dockerfile path, build context, publish directory, and build stage.
Configures the application to use a GitHub repository as its source, setting the repository, branch, owner, and build path.
Configures the application to use a GitLab repository as its source, setting the repository, branch, owner, build path, and project ID.
Configures the application to use a Bitbucket repository as its source, setting the repository, branch, owner, and build path.
Configures the application to use a Gitea repository as its source, setting the repository, branch, owner, and build path.
Configures the application to use a Docker image as its source, setting the image name, registry URL, and optional credentials.
Configures the application to use a custom Git repository URL as its source, with optional SSH key authentication.
Removes all git provider configuration from the application, resetting source type to default and clearing repository, branch, and owner fields for all providers.
Sets the application status to running. Used to indicate that a deployment is in progress.
Updates the general configuration of an application such as name, description, memory limits, CPU limits, and other settings.
Regenerates the webhook refresh token for the application, invalidating the previous token used for triggering deployments.
Triggers a new deployment for the application. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.
Removes all pending deployment jobs from the queue for the specified application.
Removes old deployment logs and artifacts for the application to free up disk space.
Forcefully terminates the currently running Docker build process for the application.

get.description: Reads the current Traefik reverse proxy configuration file for the application. Supports both local and remote server configurations.

post.description: Deploys an application from an uploaded zip file. Unzips the file into the application directory and triggers a deployment.
Writes a new Traefik reverse proxy configuration for the application. Supports both local and remote server configurations.

get.description: Retrieves CPU and memory monitoring statistics for the application. Only available in self-hosted mode.

post.description: Moves an application to a different environment within the same project or to another project's environment.
Cancels an in-progress deployment for the application and resets its status to idle. Only available in cloud version.

get.description: Searches applications by name, appName, description, repository, owner, or Docker image with pagination. Respects service-level access control.
Retrieves Docker container logs for the application with configurable tail length, time range, and optional text search filtering.

post.description: Creates a new backup configuration for a database or compose service. If enabled, automatically schedules the backup according to the provided cron expression.

get.description: Returns the details of a specific backup configuration by its ID.

post.description: Updates an existing backup configuration. Reschedules or removes the backup job depending on the enabled state.
Permanently removes a backup configuration and unschedules any associated backup job.
Immediately executes a PostgreSQL backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.
Immediately executes a MySQL backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.
Immediately executes a MariaDB backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.
Immediately executes a Compose service backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.
Immediately executes a MongoDB backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.
Immediately executes a LibSQL backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.

post.summary: Run a web server backup manually
post.description: Immediately executes a web server backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.

get.description: Lists backup files stored in the S3 destination bucket. Supports searching by path prefix and returns up to 100 results.

post.description: Creates a new Bitbucket provider configuration linked to the active organization. Requires gitProviders create permission.

get.description: Returns a single Bitbucket provider configuration by its ID.
Returns all Bitbucket providers accessible to the current user within the active organization.
Fetches the list of repositories accessible by the Bitbucket provider. Calls the Bitbucket API using the provider's credentials.
Fetches the list of branches for a specific Bitbucket repository. Calls the Bitbucket API using the provider's credentials.

post.description: Tests the connection to a Bitbucket provider by attempting to fetch its repositories. Returns the number of repositories found or throws an error on failure.
Updates a Bitbucket provider configuration. Requires gitProviders create permission.
Creates a new SSL/TLS certificate. In cloud mode, a server must be specified. Logs an audit entry upon creation.

get.description: Returns a single certificate by its ID. Verifies that the certificate belongs to the current organization.

post.description: Deletes a certificate by its ID after verifying organization ownership. Logs an audit entry before removal.

get.description: Returns all certificates belonging to the current organization, including their associated server information.

post.description: Updates the name, certificate data, and private key of an existing certificate. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.

get.description: Retrieves all nodes in the Docker Swarm cluster. Optionally targets a remote server.

post.description: Drains and forcefully removes a worker node from the Docker Swarm cluster. An audit log entry is created for the removal.

get.description: Returns the Docker Swarm join command and token for adding a new worker node to the cluster, along with the Docker version.
Returns the Docker Swarm join command and token for adding a new manager node to the cluster, along with the Docker version.

post.description: Creates a new Docker Compose service in the specified project environment with the given configuration.

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a compose service by its ID, including git provider access status and deployment configuration.

post.description: Updates the configuration of a compose service such as name, description, compose file content, and other settings.
Updates the environment variables for a compose service.
Permanently deletes a compose service and cleans up associated Docker resources, deployments, and directories. Optionally deletes associated volumes.
Removes all pending deployment jobs from the queue for the specified compose service.
Removes old deployment logs and artifacts for the compose service to free up disk space.
Forcefully terminates the currently running Docker build process for the compose service.

get.description: Parses the compose file and returns the list of services defined in it, with their current container status.
Retrieves the Docker volume mounts for a specific service within a compose stack by inspecting the running container.

post.description: Clones the compose repository from the configured git provider and returns the source type. Executes the clone command locally or on a remote server.
Adds a random suffix to service names and volumes in the compose file to avoid naming conflicts between deployments.
Randomizes the compose file for isolated deployment mode, ensuring unique service and volume names to support parallel deployments.

get.description: Returns the compose file with domains injected as Traefik labels, converted to YAML format ready for deployment.

post.description: Triggers a new deployment for the compose service. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.
Triggers a rebuild and redeployment of the compose service. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.
Stops all running containers for the compose service using docker compose stop.
Starts all containers for the compose service using docker compose start.

get.description: Generates and returns the default docker compose command that would be used to deploy the service.

post.description: Regenerates the webhook refresh token for the compose service, invalidating the previous token used for triggering deployments.
Creates a new compose service from a template by fetching its files, processing variables, creating mounts and domains, and setting up the compose configuration.

get.description: Fetches the list of available compose templates from the GitHub templates repository.
Fetches all unique tags from the available compose templates for filtering purposes.

post.description: Removes all git provider configuration from the compose service, resetting source type to default and clearing repository, branch, and owner fields for all providers.
Moves a compose service to a different environment within the same project or to another project's environment.
Processes a base64-encoded template configuration, resolving variables and generating the compose file and environment settings without applying them.
Imports a base64-encoded template into an existing compose service, replacing its compose file, environment variables, mounts, and domains with the template's configuration.
Cancels an in-progress deployment for the compose service and resets its status to idle. Only available in cloud version.

get.description: Searches compose services by name, appName, or description with pagination. Respects service-level access control.
Retrieves Docker container logs for a specific container within the compose service with configurable tail length, time range, and optional text search filtering.
Returns all deployments associated with the given application, ordered by creation date.
Returns all deployments associated with the given compose service.
Returns all deployments associated with the given server.
Returns all deployments across all services in the organization. Non-admin users only see deployments for their accessible services.
Returns all jobs in the deployment queue with their current state, timestamps, and resolved service paths.
Returns all deployments for a given service ID and type (application, compose, etc.), including associated rollback information.

post.description: Kills the running process of a deployment by sending SIGKILL to its PID. Updates the deployment status to error.
Permanently removes a deployment record and its associated data.

get.description: Reads the build/deployment log file for a specific deployment. Returns the last N lines (default 200). Works for both local and remote server deployments.

post.description: Creates a new S3-compatible backup destination for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Tests connectivity to an S3-compatible bucket using rclone. Runs locally or on a remote server depending on configuration.

get.description: Returns a single backup destination by ID. Verifies the caller belongs to the same organization.
Returns all S3-compatible backup destinations for the current organization, ordered by creation date descending.

post.description: Removes a backup destination by ID. Verifies organization ownership and logs an audit event before deletion.
Updates an existing backup destination. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes and logs an audit event.

get.description: Retrieves a list of all Docker containers. Optionally targets a specific remote server by ID.

post.description: Restarts a Docker container by its ID. An audit log entry is created for the action.
Removes a Docker container by its ID. Optionally targets a remote server. An audit log entry is created for the deletion.

get.description: Retrieves the configuration (inspect data) for a specific Docker container. Optionally targets a remote server.

get.summary: Get containers by app name match
get.description: Retrieves containers whose names match the given application name. Supports filtering by app type (stack or docker-compose) and optionally targets a remote server.
Retrieves containers filtered by application label. Supports standalone and swarm deployment types, and optionally targets a remote server.

get.summary: Get stack containers by app name
get.description: Retrieves all containers belonging to a Docker stack by application name. Optionally targets a remote server.

get.summary: Get service containers by app name
get.description: Retrieves all containers belonging to a Docker Swarm service by application name. Optionally targets a remote server.

post.summary: Upload a file to a Docker container
post.description: Uploads a file to a specified path inside a Docker container. The file is converted to a buffer and transferred to the container's filesystem.
Creates a new domain for an application or compose service. Validates permissions and logs an audit entry.

get.description: Returns all domains associated with a given application ID.
Returns all domains associated with a given compose service ID.

post.description: Generates a free traefik.me domain for an application, using the server IP to create a wildcard subdomain.

get.description: Checks whether traefik.me domains can be generated by returning the server IP address. Returns the IP from the server record or web server settings.

post.description: Updates a domain's configuration and refreshes the Traefik routing rules for the associated application or preview deployment.

get.description: Returns a single domain by its ID. Validates read permissions against the associated service or preview deployment.

post.description: Deletes a domain by its ID and removes the associated Traefik routing configuration for the application.
Checks whether a domain's DNS records are correctly configured, optionally verifying against a specific server IP.
Creates a new Gitea provider configuration linked to the active organization. Requires gitProviders create permission.

get.description: Returns a single Gitea provider configuration by its ID.
Returns all Gitea providers accessible to the current user within the active organization, filtered to only those with valid credentials.
Fetches the list of repositories accessible by the Gitea provider. Calls the Gitea API using the provider's credentials.
Fetches the list of branches for a specific Gitea repository. Calls the Gitea API using the provider's credentials.

post.description: Tests the connection to a Gitea provider by attempting to fetch its repositories. Returns the number of repositories found or throws an error on failure.
Updates a Gitea provider configuration and its associated git provider record. Requires gitProviders create permission.

get.description: Returns the base URL of the Gitea instance associated with the given provider ID.
Returns all git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Gitea) accessible to the current user within the active organization, ordered by creation date.

post.description: Toggles whether a git provider is shared with the entire organization. Only the owner of the provider can change this setting.

get.description: Returns a minimal list of all git providers in the organization for use in permission assignment UIs. Requires a valid enterprise license and member update permission.

post.description: Deletes a git provider from the organization. Requires gitProviders delete permission and the provider must belong to the active organization.

get.description: Returns a single GitHub provider configuration by its ID.
Fetches the list of repositories accessible by the GitHub provider. Calls the GitHub API using the provider's credentials.
Fetches the list of branches for a specific GitHub repository. Calls the GitHub API using the provider's credentials.
Returns all GitHub providers accessible to the current user within the active organization, filtered to only those with valid credentials.

post.description: Tests the connection to a GitHub provider by attempting to fetch its repositories. Returns the number of repositories found or throws an error on failure.
Updates a GitHub provider configuration and its associated git provider record. Requires gitProviders create permission.
Creates a new GitLab provider configuration linked to the active organization. Requires gitProviders create permission.

get.description: Returns a single GitLab provider configuration by its ID.
Returns all GitLab providers accessible to the current user within the active organization, filtered to only those with valid credentials.
Fetches the list of repositories accessible by the GitLab provider. Calls the GitLab API using the provider's credentials.
Fetches the list of branches for a specific GitLab repository. Calls the GitLab API using the provider's credentials.

post.description: Tests the connection to a GitLab provider by attempting to fetch its repositories. Returns the number of repositories found or throws an error on failure.
Updates a GitLab provider configuration and its associated git provider record. Requires gitProviders create permission.
Creates a new LibSQL database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.

get.summary: Get a LibSQL database by ID
get.description: Returns the full details of a LibSQL database service, including its environment and project configuration.

post.description: Starts the Docker container for the specified LibSQL database and sets its status to done.
Stops the Docker container for the specified LibSQL database and sets its status to idle.

post.summary: Save the external ports for a LibSQL database
post.description: Updates the external port mappings (HTTP, gRPC, admin) for the LibSQL database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that ports are not already in use.
Triggers a deployment for the specified LibSQL database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.
Updates the application status of a LibSQL database without starting or stopping the container.
Removes the LibSQL database service, its Docker container, and deletes the database record.

post.summary: Save environment variables for a LibSQL database
post.description: Updates the environment variables for the specified LibSQL database service.
Restarts the LibSQL database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.
Updates the configuration of an existing LibSQL database service.

post.summary: Move a LibSQL database to another environment
post.description: Moves the LibSQL database to a different environment within the same project.
Rebuilds the LibSQL database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.

get.description: Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified LibSQL database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.

post.description: Creates a new MariaDB database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.

get.summary: Get a MariaDB database by ID
get.description: Returns the full details of a MariaDB database service, including its environment and project configuration.

post.description: Starts the Docker container for the specified MariaDB database and sets its status to done.
Stops the Docker container for the specified MariaDB database and sets its status to idle.

post.summary: Save the external port for a MariaDB database
post.description: Updates the external port mapping for the MariaDB database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that the port is not already in use.
Triggers a deployment for the specified MariaDB database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.
Updates the application status of a MariaDB database without starting or stopping the container.
Removes the MariaDB database service, its Docker container, cancels associated backup jobs, and deletes the database record.

post.summary: Save environment variables for a MariaDB database
post.description: Updates the environment variables for the specified MariaDB database service.
Restarts the MariaDB database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.
Updates the configuration of an existing MariaDB database service.
Changes the password for a MariaDB user or root account by executing ALTER USER inside the running container and updating the stored password.

post.summary: Move a MariaDB database to another environment
post.description: Moves the MariaDB database to a different environment within the same project.
Rebuilds the MariaDB database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of MariaDB databases matching the given filters. Supports searching by name, appName, description, project, and environment.
Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified MariaDB database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.

post.description: Creates a new MongoDB database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.

get.summary: Get a MongoDB database by ID
get.description: Returns the full details of a MongoDB database service, including its environment and project configuration.

post.description: Starts the Docker container for the specified MongoDB database and sets its status to done.
Stops the Docker container for the specified MongoDB database and sets its status to idle.

post.summary: Save the external port for a MongoDB database
post.description: Updates the external port mapping for the MongoDB database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that the port is not already in use.
Triggers a deployment for the specified MongoDB database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.
Updates the application status of a MongoDB database without starting or stopping the container.
Restarts the MongoDB database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.
Removes the MongoDB database service, its Docker container, cancels associated backup jobs, and deletes the database record.

post.summary: Save environment variables for a MongoDB database
post.description: Updates the environment variables for the specified MongoDB database service.
Updates the configuration of an existing MongoDB database service.
Changes the password for the MongoDB database user by executing changeUserPassword via mongosh inside the running container and updating the stored password.

post.summary: Move a MongoDB database to another environment
post.description: Moves the MongoDB database to a different environment within the same project.
Rebuilds the MongoDB database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of MongoDB databases matching the given filters. Supports searching by name, appName, description, project, and environment.
Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified MongoDB database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.

post.description: Creates a new volume, bind, or file mount for a service. Checks service-level volume permissions and logs an audit event.
Removes a mount by ID. Resolves the owning service to check volume delete permissions and logs an audit event.

get.description: Returns a single mount by ID. Resolves the owning service to check volume read permissions.

post.description: Updates an existing mount. Resolves the owning service to check volume create permissions and logs an audit event.

get.description: Returns Docker named volumes attached to the running container of a given application. Inspects the live container to retrieve mount information.
Returns all configured mounts for a given service (application, compose, or database). Verifies service access and organization ownership.

post.description: Creates a new MySQL database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.

get.summary: Get a MySQL database by ID
get.description: Returns the full details of a MySQL database service, including its environment and project configuration.

post.description: Starts the Docker container for the specified MySQL database and sets its status to done.
Stops the Docker container for the specified MySQL database and sets its status to idle.

post.summary: Save the external port for a MySQL database
post.description: Updates the external port mapping for the MySQL database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that the port is not already in use.
Triggers a deployment for the specified MySQL database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.
Updates the application status of a MySQL database without starting or stopping the container.
Restarts the MySQL database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.
Removes the MySQL database service, its Docker container, cancels associated backup jobs, and deletes the database record.

post.summary: Save environment variables for a MySQL database
post.description: Updates the environment variables for the specified MySQL database service.
Updates the configuration of an existing MySQL database service.
Changes the password for a MySQL user or root account by executing ALTER USER inside the running container and updating the stored password.

post.summary: Move a MySQL database to another environment
post.description: Moves the MySQL database to a different environment within the same project.
Rebuilds the MySQL database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of MySQL databases matching the given filters. Supports searching by name, appName, description, project, and environment.
Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified MySQL database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.

post.description: Creates a new Slack notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Slack notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test message to the configured Slack channel to verify the webhook connection works.
Creates a new Telegram notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Telegram notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test message to the configured Telegram chat to verify the bot token and chat ID work.
Creates a new Discord notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Discord notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test embed message to the configured Discord webhook to verify the connection works.
Creates a new SMTP email notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing SMTP email notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test email via the configured SMTP settings to verify the connection works.
Creates a new Resend email notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Resend email notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test email via Resend to verify the API key and configuration work.
Removes a notification provider by ID. Verifies organization ownership and logs an audit event before deletion.

get.description: Returns a single notification provider by ID. Verifies the caller belongs to the same organization.
Returns all notification providers for the current organization, including all provider-specific details (Slack, Telegram, Discord, etc.).

post.description: Public endpoint that receives CPU/memory threshold alerts from Dokploy or remote servers. Validates the token and dispatches notifications to all configured providers.
Creates a new Gotify notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Gotify notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test notification to the configured Gotify server to verify the connection works.
Creates a new ntfy notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing ntfy notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test notification to the configured ntfy topic to verify the connection works.
Creates a new Mattermost notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Mattermost notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test message to the configured Mattermost webhook to verify the connection works.
Creates a new custom webhook notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing custom webhook notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test payload to the configured custom webhook URL to verify the connection works.
Creates a new Lark notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Lark notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test message to the configured Lark webhook to verify the connection works.
Creates a new Microsoft Teams notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Microsoft Teams notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test message to the configured Microsoft Teams webhook to verify the connection works.
Creates a new Pushover notification provider for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Updates an existing Pushover notification provider. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.
Sends a test notification to the configured Pushover account to verify the connection works.

get.description: Returns all notification providers that support email (SMTP and Resend) for the current organization.

post.description: Creates a new port mapping for an application, binding a published port to a target port. Logs an audit entry.

get.description: Returns a single port mapping by its ID, including the associated application details.

post.description: Deletes a port mapping by its ID and logs an audit entry with the published and target port details.
Updates an existing port mapping's configuration and logs an audit entry.
Creates a new PostgreSQL database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.

get.summary: Get a PostgreSQL database by ID
get.description: Returns the full details of a PostgreSQL database service, including its environment and project configuration.

post.description: Starts the Docker container for the specified PostgreSQL database and sets its status to done.
Stops the Docker container for the specified PostgreSQL database and sets its status to idle.

post.summary: Save the external port for a PostgreSQL database
post.description: Updates the external port mapping for the PostgreSQL database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that the port is not already in use.
Triggers a deployment for the specified PostgreSQL database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.
Updates the application status of a PostgreSQL database without starting or stopping the container.
Removes the PostgreSQL database service, its Docker container, cancels associated backup jobs, and deletes the database record.

post.summary: Save environment variables for a PostgreSQL database
post.description: Updates the environment variables for the specified PostgreSQL database service.
Restarts the PostgreSQL database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.
Updates the configuration of an existing PostgreSQL database service.
Changes the password for the PostgreSQL database user by executing ALTER USER inside the running container and updating the stored password.

post.summary: Move a PostgreSQL database to another environment
post.description: Moves the PostgreSQL database to a different environment within the same project.
Rebuilds the PostgreSQL database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of PostgreSQL databases matching the given filters. Supports searching by name, appName, description, project, and environment.
Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified PostgreSQL database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.
Returns all preview deployments associated with the given application.
Returns the details of a specific preview deployment by its ID.

post.description: Permanently removes a preview deployment and its associated resources.
Triggers a rebuild of an existing preview deployment by adding a new job to the deployment queue.
Creates a new project in the current organization with a default environment. Validates server availability for cloud deployments.

get.description: Retrieves a project by its ID with all environments and services. Filters services based on the user's access permissions.
Returns all projects in the current organization with their environments and services. Filters results based on the user's access permissions.
Returns all projects with their environments and services for the permissions management UI. Requires member update permission.
Searches projects by name or description with pagination. Respects project-level access control for non-admin users.

post.description: Permanently deletes a project and all its associated environments, services, and resources.
Updates a project's name, description, or environment variables. Validates organization ownership and project-level access permissions.
Duplicates services from a source environment into a new project or into the same project. Copies applications, compose services, databases, and their associated domains, mounts, ports, and backups.
Creates a new redirect rule for an application using a regex pattern. Logs an audit entry.

get.description: Returns a single redirect rule by its ID.

post.description: Deletes a redirect rule by its ID and logs an audit entry.
Updates an existing redirect rule's configuration and logs an audit entry.
Creates a new Redis database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.

get.summary: Get a Redis database by ID
get.description: Returns the full details of a Redis database service, including its environment and project configuration.

post.description: Starts the Docker container for the specified Redis database and sets its status to done.
Restarts the Redis database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.
Stops the Docker container for the specified Redis database and sets its status to idle.

post.summary: Save the external port for a Redis database
post.description: Updates the external port mapping for the Redis database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that the port is not already in use.
Triggers a deployment for the specified Redis database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.
Updates the application status of a Redis database without starting or stopping the container.
Removes the Redis database service, its Docker container, and deletes the database record.

post.summary: Save environment variables for a Redis database
post.description: Updates the environment variables for the specified Redis database service.
Updates the configuration of an existing Redis database service.
Changes the password for the Redis database by executing CONFIG SET requirepass inside the running container and updating the stored password.

post.summary: Move a Redis database to another environment
post.description: Moves the Redis database to a different environment within the same project.
Rebuilds the Redis database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of Redis databases matching the given filters. Supports searching by name, appName, description, project, and environment.
Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified Redis database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.

post.description: Creates a new Docker registry entry for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Removes a Docker registry entry by ID. Verifies organization ownership and logs an audit event before deletion.
Updates an existing Docker registry entry. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes and logs an audit event.

get.description: Returns all Docker registry entries for the current organization.
Returns a single Docker registry entry by ID. Verifies the caller belongs to the same organization.

post.description: Attempts a docker login with the provided credentials to verify the registry URL, username, and password are valid. Can run locally or on a remote server.
Looks up a saved registry by ID and attempts a docker login with its stored credentials. Verifies organization ownership before testing.
Creates a new HTTP basic auth security entry for an application with the provided username and password. Logs an audit entry.

get.description: Returns a single HTTP basic auth security entry by its ID.

post.description: Deletes an HTTP basic auth security entry by its ID and logs an audit entry.
Updates an existing HTTP basic auth security entry's configuration and logs an audit entry.
Creates a new server in the organization. In cloud mode, enforces the user's server quantity limit. Returns the newly created server.

get.description: Retrieves a single server by its ID. Validates that the user has access to the server within their organization.
Returns the default setup command for a server. The command varies depending on whether the server is a build server or a deploy server.
Returns all servers in the current organization along with a count of associated services (applications, compose, databases). Results are filtered by the user's accessible server permissions.
Returns a minimal list of servers (ID, name, IP, type) used for configuring member permissions. Requires a valid enterprise license.
Returns the total number of servers across all organizations owned by the current user.
Returns all deploy-type servers that have an SSH key configured. In cloud mode, only active servers are included. Results are filtered by the user's accessible server permissions.
Returns all build-type servers that have an SSH key configured. In cloud mode, only active servers are included. Results are filtered by the user's accessible server permissions.

post.description: Runs the initial setup process on a remote server, installing required dependencies and configuring Docker. An audit log entry is created.

get.description: Checks the server for required tools and configuration including Docker, Rclone, Nixpacks, Buildpacks, Railpack, Swarm mode, network setup, and privilege mode.
Performs a security audit on the server, checking UFW firewall, SSH configuration, non-root user setup, unattended upgrades, and Fail2Ban status.

post.description: Configures and deploys the monitoring agent on a server with the specified metrics configuration including refresh rates, retention, thresholds, and container service filters.
Deletes a server and removes all associated deployments. Fails if the server has active services. In cloud mode, updates the user's server quantity allocation.
Updates the configuration of an existing server. Fails if the server is inactive. An audit log entry is created for the update.

get.description: Returns the public IP address of the local server. Returns an empty string in cloud mode.
Returns the current server time and timezone. Returns null in cloud mode.
Fetches monitoring metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network) from the server's monitoring agent endpoint. Requires the monitoring service to be configured and running.
Retrieve the current web server settings. Returns null on cloud.

post.description: Reload the Dokploy Docker service with the current version. Disabled on cloud.
Execute FLUSHALL on the Dokploy Redis container, removing all cached data. Disabled on cloud.
Force-reload the Dokploy Redis Docker service. Disabled on cloud.
Remove all pending jobs from the deployment queue. Disabled on cloud.
Force-reload the Dokploy Traefik Docker service. Runs in the background to avoid proxy timeouts. Optionally targets a specific server.
Enable or disable the Traefik dashboard by adding or removing port 8080. Checks for port conflicts before enabling. Runs the Traefik setup in the background.
Remove all unused Docker images from the host or a specified server.
Remove all unused Docker volumes from the host or a specified server.
Remove all stopped Docker containers from the host or a specified server.
Remove Docker builder cache from the host or a specified server.
Run a full Docker system prune and builder cache cleanup on the host or a specified server.
Run a comprehensive Docker cleanup (images, containers, volumes, builders, system prune) in the background to avoid gateway timeouts.
Delete and recreate the monitoring data directory, removing all collected metrics. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Retrieve Docker disk usage statistics. Returns an empty array on cloud.

post.description: Store an SSH private key in the web server settings for remote server access. Disabled on cloud.
Configure the server domain, HTTPS settings, certificate type, and Let's Encrypt email. Updates both web server settings and Traefik configuration. Disabled on cloud.
Clear the stored SSH private key from web server settings. Disabled on cloud.
Enable or disable automatic Docker cleanup for a server or the web server. When enabled, schedules a cron job that periodically removes unused Docker resources and sends notifications.

get.description: Read the main Traefik configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

post.description: Overwrite the main Traefik configuration file with the provided content. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Read the Dokploy-specific Traefik configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

post.description: Overwrite the Dokploy-specific Traefik configuration file with the provided content. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Read the Traefik middlewares configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

post.description: Overwrite the Traefik middlewares configuration file with the provided content. Disabled on cloud.
Check whether a newer version of Dokploy is available. Returns default data on cloud.
Pull the latest Dokploy Docker image and update the service if a new version is available. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Return the currently running Dokploy version from package.json.
Return the Docker image tag of the running Dokploy instance.
Read the directory listing of the main Traefik configuration path. Requires traefikFiles.read permission.

post.description: Write content to a specific Traefik configuration file at the given path. Requires traefikFiles.write permission.

get.description: Read the content of a specific Traefik configuration file at the given path. Requires traefikFiles.read permission. Validates server ownership when a serverId is provided.
Return the configured server IP address from web server settings. Returns an empty string on cloud.

post.description: Update the server IP address stored in web server settings. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Generate and return the full OpenAPI document for the Dokploy API, including all endpoints, tags, and security schemes.
Retrieve the environment variables configured for the Dokploy Traefik service, optionally for a specific server.

post.description: Update the environment variables for the Dokploy Traefik service and restart it in the background. Preserves the current port configuration.

get.description: Check whether port 8080 (Traefik dashboard) is currently enabled in the Traefik service port configuration.

get.summary: Check if request logging is active
get.description: Check whether Traefik access log (request logging) is enabled by inspecting the main Traefik configuration. Returns true on cloud.

post.description: Enable or disable Traefik access log (request logging) by updating the main Traefik configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Return whether this Dokploy instance is running in cloud mode.
Check whether the current organization has any servers or projects, indicating active usage.
Verify the application is running and the database connection is healthy by executing a simple query.
Check the health status of PostgreSQL, Redis, and Traefik services. Returns healthy for all on cloud.

post.description: Install and configure GPU support (NVIDIA runtime) on the host or a specified server. On cloud, a serverId is required.

get.description: Retrieve detailed GPU status including driver version, CUDA support, memory info, available GPUs, and Docker Swarm GPU resources for the host or a specified server.

post.description: Replace the Traefik service port mappings with the provided list. Checks for port conflicts before applying. Runs the Traefik setup in the background.

get.description: Retrieve the current port mappings configured for the Dokploy Traefik service.

post.description: Start or stop the automatic log cleanup cron job. Provide a cron expression to start, or null to stop. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Return the current status and schedule of the automatic log cleanup job.
Return the list of Dokploy cloud IP addresses from the environment configuration. Returns an empty array in self-hosted mode.

post.description: Stores a new SSH key for the current organization and logs an audit event.
Removes an SSH key by ID. Verifies organization ownership and logs an audit event before deletion.

get.description: Returns a single SSH key by ID. Verifies the caller belongs to the same organization.
Returns all SSH keys for the current organization, ordered by creation date descending.

get.summary: List SSH keys for app selection
get.description: Returns a lightweight list of SSH keys (ID and name only) for the current organization, suitable for dropdown selectors in application forms.

post.description: Generates a new SSH key pair of the specified type (RSA, ED25519, etc.) and returns both public and private keys.
Updates an existing SSH key. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes and logs an audit event.

get.description: Returns the active Stripe billing plan (hobby, startup, or legacy) for the caller's organization owner. Returns null if not on cloud or no subscription exists.
Returns available Stripe products, the user's active subscriptions, current plan tier, billing interval, and price amount.

post.description: Creates a Stripe checkout session for subscribing to a billing plan. Handles customer creation or reuse and returns the session ID for redirect.
Creates a Stripe billing portal session URL so the user can manage their subscription, payment methods, and invoices.
Upgrades or changes the current Stripe subscription to a different tier or server quantity. Applies prorated charges for the billing period change.

get.description: Returns whether the organization can create more servers based on their subscription's server quantity limit. Always returns true for self-hosted instances.

post.description: Enables or disables email notifications for invoice events. Only available on Dokploy Cloud.

get.description: Returns up to 100 Stripe invoices for the organization owner, including status, amounts, and download links.
Retrieves all nodes in the Docker Swarm. Optionally targets a remote server by ID.
Retrieves detailed information about a specific Docker Swarm node by its node ID. Optionally targets a remote server.
Retrieves all applications (services) running across Docker Swarm nodes. Optionally targets a remote server.
Retrieves resource usage statistics for all containers. Optionally targets a remote server and validates organization access.
Retrieve all members of the current active organization, including their associated user data, ordered by creation date.

Retrieve a specific user's membership and profile within the active organization. Users can view their own data; admins and owners can view any member. Requires member.update permission for non-self lookups.
Return the current user's ID and active organization ID from the session. Returns null if no valid session exists.
Retrieve the current authenticated user's membership record including user profile and API keys for the active organization.
Return the fully resolved permissions for the current user in the active organization, combining role-based and custom permissions.
Check whether the current user has root admin access. Only returns true in cloud mode for the designated admin user or impersonating sessions.
Retrieve the current admin user's backup configurations including destinations, deployments, and API keys.
Retrieve the user record associated with server metrics access for the current organization membership.

post.description: Update the current user's profile. If changing the password, the current password must be provided and verified. Logs an audit event on success.

get.description: Look up a user by their authentication token. This is a public endpoint that does not require an active session.
Retrieve the server IP, paid features flag, and monitoring configuration needed for metrics collection.

post.description: Delete a user from the organization. Only owners and admins can remove users; owners cannot be removed, and admins cannot remove themselves or other admins. Disabled on cloud.
Update permissions for a specific member in the organization. Only the organization owner can assign permissions. Git provider and server access restrictions require a valid license.

get.summary: Get pending invitations for current user
get.description: Retrieve all pending organization invitations for the current user's email that have not yet expired.
Fetch monitoring metrics for a specific container by querying the metrics endpoint. Requires an application name, metrics URL, and authentication token.

post.description: Generate a new authentication token for the current user.
Delete an API key by ID. Only the owner of the API key can delete it. Logs an audit event on success.
Create a new API key for the current user, scoped to a specific organization. Supports optional rate limiting and request limiting configuration.

get.description: Return the number of organizations a user belongs to. Users can check their own count; admins and owners can check counts for members in the active organization.

post.description: Create a new user with email and password and add them to the active organization with the specified role. Only available in self-hosted mode.
Send an invitation email to a pending invitee using a configured email or Resend notification provider. Returns the generated invite link. Disabled on cloud.

get.description: Retrieve the list of template IDs that the current user has bookmarked.

post.description: Add or remove a template from the current user's bookmarks. Returns whether the template is now bookmarked.

get.description: Returns a single AI provider configuration by its ID.
Fetches the list of models from the given AI provider URL. Supports OpenAI-compatible, Ollama, Gemini, Perplexity, ZAI, and MiniMax providers.

post.description: Saves a new AI provider configuration (API URL, key, model) for the current organization.
Updates an existing AI provider configuration for the current organization.

get.description: Returns all AI provider configurations for the current organization.
Returns a single AI provider configuration by its ID.

post.description: Removes an AI provider configuration by its ID.

get.description: Returns a lightweight list of enabled AI providers (ID, name, model) for the current organization, suitable for dropdown selectors.

post.description: Sends build or runtime logs to the specified AI provider for analysis. Returns a summary of issues found, root causes, and suggested fixes.
Sends a minimal prompt to the specified AI provider and model to verify the API URL, key, and model are valid and reachable.
Uses AI to generate deployment configuration suggestions (docker-compose variants) based on the user's input prompt.
Deploys an AI-generated suggestion by creating a compose service with its docker-compose file, environment variables, domains, and config file mounts.
Create a new organization and add the current user as the owner. Only owners and admins can create organizations in self-hosted mode.

get.description: Retrieve all organizations the current user is a member of, including their membership details.
Retrieve a single organization by its ID. The current user must be a member of the organization.

post.description: Update the name and logo of an organization. Only the organization owner can perform this action.
Delete an organization by ID. Only the owner can delete it, and they must retain at least one organization.
Create a pending invitation for a user by email to join the active organization with the specified role. Checks for existing membership and pending invitations. Supports custom roles.

get.description: Retrieve all invitations for the active organization, ordered by status and expiration date.

post.description: Delete a pending invitation by ID. Only invitations belonging to the active organization can be removed.
Change the role of a member in the active organization. Users cannot change their own role, and the owner role is nontransferable. Only owners can change admin roles. Supports custom roles.
Set an organization as the default for the current user. Unsets any previous default and marks the specified organization as the new default.

get.description: Retrieve the organization that is currently active in the user's session. Returns null if no organization is active.

post.description: Creates a new scheduled job for an application or compose service. If enabled, the job is automatically scheduled using the provided cron expression and timezone.
Updates an existing scheduled job configuration. Reschedules or removes the job depending on the enabled state.
Permanently removes a scheduled job and unschedules any associated cron job.

get.description: Returns all scheduled jobs for a given service (application, compose, server, or dokploy-server), including their deployment history.
Returns the details of a specific scheduled job by its ID.

post.description: Immediately executes a scheduled job outside of its normal cron schedule.
Permanently removes a rollback record by its ID.
Rolls back an application to a previous deployment by restoring its Docker image and redeploying.

get.description: Returns all volume backup configurations for a given service, including related service details.

post.description: Creates a new volume backup configuration for a service. If enabled, automatically schedules the backup using the provided cron expression.

get.description: Returns the details of a specific volume backup configuration by its ID.

post.description: Permanently removes a volume backup configuration by its ID.
Updates an existing volume backup configuration. Reschedules or removes the backup job depending on the enabled state.
Immediately executes a volume backup outside of its normal cron schedule.
Creates a new environment within a project. The name 'production' is reserved and cannot be used. Checks creation permissions and logs an audit event.

get.description: Returns a single environment by ID with all its services. Non-admin users only see services they have been granted access to.
Returns all environments for a given project. Non-admin users only see environments and services they have been granted access to.

post.description: Deletes an environment by ID. The default environment cannot be deleted. Checks deletion permissions and environment access before removing.
Updates an environment's name, description, or env variables. The default environment cannot be renamed. Checks environment access and env-var write permissions.
Creates a copy of an existing environment including its services. Checks environment access and organization ownership before duplicating.

get.description: Searches environments by name, description, or project with pagination. Non-admin users only see environments they have been granted access to.

post.description: Creates a new tag with a name and color for the current organization. Tag names must be unique within the organization.

get.description: Returns all tags for the current organization, ordered alphabetically by name.
Returns a single tag by ID. Only returns tags belonging to the caller's organization.

post.description: Updates an existing tag's name and/or color. Verifies the tag belongs to the caller's organization. Tag names must remain unique.
Deletes a tag by ID. Cascade-deletes all project-tag associations. Verifies the tag belongs to the caller's organization.
Associates a tag with a project. Verifies that both the tag and project belong to the caller's organization and that the caller has project access.
Removes a tag-project association. Verifies that both the tag and project belong to the caller's organization and that the caller has project access.
Replaces all tag associations for a project with the provided list of tag IDs. Removes existing associations first, then inserts the new set.
Creates a new file patch for an application or compose service. Checks service-level permissions and logs an audit event.

get.description: Returns a single patch by ID. Resolves the associated service to verify read permissions.
Returns all patches associated with a given application or compose service.

post.description: Updates the content or configuration of an existing patch. Resolves the associated service to verify permissions and logs an audit event.
Deletes a patch by ID. Resolves the associated service to verify delete permissions and logs an audit event.
Enables or disables a patch without deleting it. Resolves the associated service to verify permissions and logs an audit event.
Ensures a patch repository is initialized for the given application or compose service. Creates the repo if it does not exist and logs an audit event.

get.description: Reads the directory listing at a given path inside the patch repository for an application or compose service.
Reads a file from the patch repository. For delete-type patches it returns the current repo content; otherwise returns the patch content if available, falling back to the repo file.

post.description: Creates or updates a patch record from file content. If a patch already exists for the file path, it updates the existing patch; otherwise creates a new one.
Creates a delete-type patch that will remove the specified file from the service on next deployment. Logs an audit event.
Removes all patch repository working directories on the local or a specified remote server. Admin-only operation that logs an audit event.

issues.description: An array of issues that were responsible for the error
An array of issues that were responsible for the error
An array of issues that were responsible for the error
An array of issues that were responsible for the error
An array of issues that were responsible for the error

apiKey.name: x-api-key
apiKey.description: API key authentication. Generate an API key from your Dokploy dashboard under Settings > API Keys.
